title: A Clone Of My Own

jokes.text: Good news! The university is bringing me up on disciplinary charges.
jokes.setup: Professor's typical 'Good news!' announcement creates expectation of positive news
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself by announcing bad news with his signature cheerful delivery
jokes.explanation: Classic Farnsworth character moment - his oblivious delivery of terrible news as good news is perfectly in character and well-structured

jokes.text: Wait, that's not good news.
jokes.setup: His previous 'good news' announcement
jokes.punchline: Delayed realization that disciplinary charges are actually bad
jokes.explanation: Simple delayed realization gag that extends the previous joke effectively

jokes.text: Everyone's always in favor of saving Hitler's brain. But when you put it in the body of a great white shark? Suddenly you've gone too far!
jokes.setup: Sets up reasonable premise of saving Hitler's brain being controversial
jokes.punchline: Escalates to absurd extreme of putting it in a shark, treating it as perfectly logical progression
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation joke that perfectly captures Farnsworth's mad scientist perspective. The casual delivery of an insane concept is comedy gold.

jokes.text: Listen to me, you pompous frauds! If I'm going down, I'm taking you all with me! Dean Vernon, I know the truth! It was you driving your hover car that night, not your horse.
jokes.setup: Dramatic threat to expose secrets builds tension
jokes.punchline: Reveals absurd secret about hover car vs. horse - undermines the drama with silly revelation
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that subverts dramatic blackmail with absurd future-world detail

jokes.text: Dean Epsilon... I know all about your 'Department of Pool Boy Studies'.
jokes.setup: Continuation of blackmail threats
jokes.punchline: Implies sexual scandal through academic department name
jokes.explanation: Decent innuendo joke but relies on obvious sexual implication

jokes.text: And Dr. Wernstrum? Wernstrum!
jokes.setup: Pattern of naming and threatening people
jokes.punchline: Gets so worked up he just shouts the name twice instead of revealing a secret
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Farnsworth's hatred overwhelming his blackmail attempt

jokes.text: Actually, professor, we merely called you here to say... surprise. Happy birthday! And you, Coach Smalley! Or should I say, 'Coach Hairpiece'?
jokes.setup: Reveals the meeting was actually a surprise party
jokes.punchline: Professor continues his blackmail rant despite the pleasant surprise
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that makes Farnsworth's paranoid rant even funnier in hindsight

jokes.text: Good evening, I'm... Sorry. I'm Bender, and I'll be emceeing this roast.
jokes.setup: Bender starts to introduce himself
jokes.punchline: Apologizes for no clear reason then continues - typical Bender non-sequitur
jokes.explanation: Mild character moment showing Bender's awkward hosting style

jokes.text: They say you can judge a man by the company he keeps. So here's the professor's oldest friend, a grotesque, stinking lobster!
jokes.setup: Uses familiar saying about judging by company
jokes.punchline: Applies it to insult Zoidberg in typical Bender fashion
jokes.explanation: Solid roast joke that works within Bender's character and the roast format

jokes.text: Good evening, ladies and germs. That wasn't a joke. I was talking to Dean Streptococcus.
jokes.setup: Uses classic comedy opening 'ladies and germs'
jokes.punchline: Reveals he literally meant germs due to a character named after bacteria
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of classic comedy opening that works on multiple levels

jokes.text: Now I'm not saying Professor Farnsworth is old... but if you consider his age, he's likely to die soon.
jokes.setup: Classic roast format 'I'm not saying X but...'
jokes.punchline: Delivers the most obvious and blunt observation possible
jokes.explanation: Good example of Zoidberg's terrible comedy skills - subverts roast format by being too literal

jokes.text: Hey, Ringo? That was the joke. It's ShowTime at the Apollo all over again.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg's joke fell flat with the audience
jokes.punchline: References both Beatles member and famous venue for bad performers getting booed
jokes.explanation: Decent meta joke about Zoidberg's comedic failures with good cultural references

jokes.text: Where would a professor be without students who love and respect him? Right there!
jokes.setup: Rhetorical question about professor-student relationships
jokes.punchline: Points out that Farnsworth's students clearly don't respect him
jokes.explanation: Solid roast joke with good economy and clear structure

jokes.text: But seriously, of all the former crew members of his delivery ship... our next speaker is by far the most alive. Captain Musky?
jokes.setup: Builds up next speaker as distinguished among crew members
jokes.punchline: Reveals the distinction is simply being alive, implying others are dead
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor that reveals Farnsworth's dangerous history as ship captain

jokes.text: I wouldn't want to follow that guy.
jokes.setup: Captain Musky presumably gives a terrible or disturbing speech
jokes.punchline: Understated reaction to whatever horrible thing was just said
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction gag that implies something disturbing happened off-screen

jokes.text: And now, a man who needs no introduction.
jokes.setup: Classic introduction phrase for famous people
jokes.punchline: Long awkward silence follows, setting up next joke
jokes.explanation: Sets up the following joke about Fry being unknown

jokes.text: Fry, get up there!
jokes.setup: Previous 'needs no introduction' line followed by silence
jokes.punchline: Reveals the person is so unknown they need to be told to come up
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the setup that plays on Fry's insignificance

jokes.text: When I was first asked to make a film about my nephew, the professor... I thought, why should I? Then later, Leela made the film. But if I had made it, there would be more topless women on motorcycles.
jokes.setup: Fry explains being asked to make a biographical film
jokes.punchline: His only creative input would be gratuitous nudity, showing his shallow priorities
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's immature perspective on filmmaking

jokes.text: But if I had made it, there would be more topless women on motorcycles.
jokes.setup: Reflecting on how his version would differ
jokes.punchline: Random juvenile fantasy element
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's juvenile mindset, though somewhat cheap humor.

jokes.text: A precocious child, he learned to read while he was still in diapers... at age 8.
jokes.setup: Sets up image of very early reading ability
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was 8 years old in diapers, subverting the 'precocious' claim
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that completely reframes the setup information

jokes.text: Soon he became a greasy teenager. Dork alert!
jokes.setup: Documentary narration about teenage years
jokes.punchline: Someone actually yells 'dork alert' at young Farnsworth, confirming his nerdiness
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective gag that reinforces Farnsworth's nerdy background

jokes.text: Fast cars, trendy nightspots, beautiful women. The professor designed them all, working out of his one-room apartment.
jokes.setup: Lists exciting lifestyle elements suggesting glamorous life
jokes.punchline: Reveals he designed these things rather than experienced them, and from a tiny apartment
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection that subverts expectations about Farnsworth's glamorous scientist life

jokes.text: Where he created the first robot capable of qualifying for a boat loan.
jokes.setup: Documentary about his robot company work
jokes.punchline: His achievement is oddly specific financial capability rather than impressive robot function
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd detail that perfectly captures bureaucratic future world priorities

jokes.text: And now, even as he nears his 150th birthday... the professor retains the fiery passion of youth. Listen to me, you pompous frauds! If I'm going down, I'm taking you all with me!
jokes.setup: Narrator claims he has youthful passion
jokes.punchline: Immediately cuts to him having the same paranoid rant from earlier
jokes.explanation: Good callback that recontextualizes his earlier paranoid behavior as 'youthful passion'

jokes.text: I said, words.
jokes.setup: Professor was asked to say a few words
jokes.punchline: Impatient correction implying he's just standing there silently
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective beat showing Farnsworth's confusion or senility

jokes.text: What a pleasure it is to see my lifetime of accomplishment... summed up in a 3-minute film. My best years are behind me. So much left undone. So little time.
jokes.setup: Expected gracious response to birthday celebration
jokes.punchline: Completely depressing and morbid speech that kills the party mood
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Farnsworth's total inability to read social situations

jokes.text: Funny, funny stuff!
jokes.setup: Farnsworth's deeply depressing speech
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt to lighten the mood after uncomfortable moment
jokes.explanation: Adds to the cringe by showing someone's desperate attempt to salvage the moment

jokes.text: Leela, he could live another 100 years! No, he couldn't.
jokes.setup: Fry's optimistic assessment of longevity
jokes.punchline: Leela's blunt immediate contradiction
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast between Fry's optimism and Leela's realism

jokes.text: When you turn 160... robots from the Sunset Squad take you to a mysterious planet... and you never return. Wow, a whole planet of old people. Where is it? Nobody knows.
jokes.setup: Explanation of futuristic elderly care system
jokes.punchline: Fry's naive enthusiasm for what's clearly a death sentence
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about euthanasia disguised as elder care, with Fry's obliviousness making it funnier

jokes.text: That damn time machine alone set me back 15 years.
jokes.setup: Professor lamenting wasted time on inventions
jokes.punchline: Time machine 'set him back' in time - literal and figurative meaning
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels - excellent economy and structure

jokes.text: If only it'd work, you could go back and not waste your time on it.
jokes.setup: Professor's regret about time machine
jokes.punchline: Fry suggests using the broken time machine to prevent making it - circular logic
jokes.explanation: Good logical paradox joke that fits Fry's character while being cleverly constructed

jokes.text: I hope he didn't die. Unless he named me his successor. Then I hope he did die.
jokes.setup: Concern about the Professor being gone
jokes.punchline: Bender's completely selfish reversal based on personal benefit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment showing his complete amorality and self-interest

jokes.text: You, the successor? Over my empty shell! The professor will pick me. Only I have his lobster-like tenacity.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg arguing for why he should be successor
jokes.punchline: Claims to share 'lobster-like tenacity' as a qualification - he's actually a lobster
jokes.explanation: Good character moment that plays on Zoidberg's species while showing his delusional confidence

jokes.text: Up yours, Zoidberg! Up wherever your species traditionally crams things.
jokes.setup: Typical insult exchange
jokes.punchline: Bender has to adjust his insult for alien anatomy he doesn't understand
jokes.explanation: Clever take on standard insults adapted for alien species - shows world-building through comedy

jokes.text: The only sensible way to pick a successor is with a limbo contest. Kingston rules. Two men go down, one comes up.
jokes.setup: Discussion of how to pick successor
jokes.punchline: Hermes suggests limbo contest with ominous 'two men go down, one comes up' rule
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hermes character moment combining his Jamaican heritage with bureaucratic absurdity and dark implications

jokes.text: Look, the professor trusts me with a giant spaceship. He wouldn't trust you with his dentures. Yes, he would.
jokes.setup: Leela makes logical argument about trust levels
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's pathetically optimistic response about dentures
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Zoidberg's delusional optimism even about tiny responsibilities

jokes.text: Sorry, everyone. But need I remind you, blood is thicker than water. Blood. Thicker. Water.
jokes.setup: Fry uses common saying about family
jokes.punchline: Repeats it word by word as if it's profound wisdom
jokes.explanation: Good Fry moment showing his tendency to treat clichés as deep insights

jokes.text: Goodbye. Professor?
jokes.setup: Professor says anyone with weak heart should leave
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg immediately leaves, then has to ask what the announcement was
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg moment showing his literal interpretation and immediate regret

jokes.text: As you all know, I am not long for this world. Yes, we know. One foot in the grave.
jokes.setup: Professor's dramatic announcement about mortality
jokes.punchline: Crew's blunt agreement kills any drama he was trying to create
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of dramatic moment through crew's matter-of-fact response

jokes.text: It's someone in whom I have great faith... though his mind is undeveloped and he's accomplished nothing.
jokes.setup: Builds up his choice with 'great faith'
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercuts it with terrible qualifications
jokes.explanation: Good contradiction structure that perfectly sets up the clone reveal

jokes.text: He's horrible! Crud. Can I at least be in charge of your dentures? You wish.
jokes.setup: Everyone's horrified reaction to the clone
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's pathetic request for denture duty is rejected
jokes.explanation: Pays off earlier dentures joke while showing Zoidberg's desperation

jokes.text: I left him in his first tube too long and he got squished against the side.
jokes.setup: Question about why clone doesn't look like him
jokes.punchline: Casual explanation of deformity due to careless cloning process
jokes.explanation: Great absurd detail that shows Farnsworth's careless approach to science

jokes.text: Is he dumb or just ugly? Let's find out.
jokes.setup: Question about the clone's abilities
jokes.punchline: Professor's casual willingness to experiment on his own clone
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing both Bender's cruelty and Farnsworth's scientific detachment.

jokes.text: What? You've never seen a genius's wiener before? No. Never. Well, once in the park.
jokes.setup: Cubert's arrogant response to staring
jokes.punchline: Awkward admission about seeing genius anatomy in inappropriate context
jokes.explanation: Crude but effective cringe humor with good uncomfortable revelation

jokes.text: I lost it. In a volcano.
jokes.setup: Question about medical degree
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously dramatic and unlikely explanation for losing credentials
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg moment - absurd excuse delivered with complete sincerity

jokes.text: Me no speak-a the English.
jokes.setup: Cubert questioning Bender's usefulness
jokes.punchline: Bender pretends not to speak English while speaking broken English
jokes.explanation: Good Bender deflection tactic showing his lazy approach to avoiding work

jokes.text: There's someone I'd like you to meet. His name is depth perception.
jokes.setup: Question about Leela having one eye
jokes.punchline: Introduces abstract concept as if it's a person she's missing
jokes.explanation: Clever personification joke with good structure and delivery

jokes.text: Why, you little... Wow, that hurt... the air!
jokes.setup: Leela goes to hit Cubert
jokes.punchline: Misses due to poor depth perception, proving his point while trying to disprove it
jokes.explanation: Perfect physical comedy that proves the joke's point through the character's reaction to it

jokes.text: Him pending para un bending.
jokes.setup: Bender's fake non-English continues
jokes.punchline: Creates fake Spanish that incorporates his actual function (bending)
jokes.explanation: Clever extension of the language gag that incorporates character-specific wordplay

jokes.text: I didn't realize you were the inventor of the junk heap.
jokes.setup: Professor showing off his inventions
jokes.punchline: Cubert's dismissive assessment of the impressive-sounding invention
jokes.explanation: Good deflating comment that shows Cubert's arrogant dismissiveness

jokes.text: That's my prizewinning Smell-a-Scope. If a dog craps anywhere in the universe, I won't be out of the loop.
jokes.setup: Defending his invention as prize-winning
jokes.punchline: Reveals its purpose is detecting dog excrement across space
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd invention that perfectly captures Farnsworth's priorities and scientific enthusiasm

jokes.text: Unfortunately, so far... it only translates into an incomprehensible dead language. Hello. Bonjour. Crazy gibberish!
jokes.setup: Universal translator that doesn't work properly
jokes.punchline: Translates English to French, which Cubert calls 'crazy gibberish'
jokes.explanation: Good satire of failed technology with added humor of French being considered gibberish

jokes.text: That's impossible. You can't go faster than the speed of light. Of course not. That's why scientists increased the speed of light in 2208.
jokes.setup: Cubert states physical law about light speed
jokes.punchline: Professor casually mentions changing fundamental physics constant
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic that perfectly captures sci-fi comedy approach to scientific problems - became a classic quote

jokes.text: It came to me in a dream and I forgot it in another dream.
jokes.setup: Asked to explain how his engine works
jokes.punchline: Completely unhelpful explanation that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Farnsworth's mad scientist character - brilliant economy and absurd logic

jokes.text: Your explanations are pure weapons-grade bologna-ium.
jokes.setup: Cubert criticizing the Professor's explanations
jokes.punchline: Creates fake scientific element name for nonsense (bologna + -ium suffix)
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that combines scientific terminology with common expression for nonsense

jokes.text: No, that's what being a magical elf is all about.
jokes.setup: Professor's claim about what being a scientist means
jokes.punchline: Cubert's dismissive categorization as fantasy rather than science
jokes.explanation: Good dismissive response that contrasts scientific method with wishful thinking

jokes.text: No, that's what being a magical elf is all about.
jokes.setup: Professor's idealistic view of science
jokes.punchline: Clone's dismissive comparison to fantasy
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between scientific idealism and practical cynicism.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! It's Elzar, the TV chef! Kill me now, people!
jokes.setup: Celebrity chef arrives at restaurant
jokes.punchline: Amy's over-the-top fan reaction requesting death from excitement
jokes.explanation: Standard celebrity worship joke with Amy's characteristic enthusiasm

jokes.text: Are you an ass-kissing machine? Yes, sir! Good one, sir!
jokes.setup: Elzar asks if Bender is sycophantic
jokes.punchline: Bender's enthusiastic agreement proves the point while missing the insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic response where character proves the accusation while defending against it

jokes.text: I don't want to be an inventor. I want to be something useful, like a teacher's aide... or a prison guard or a science-fiction cartoon writer.
jokes.setup: Cubert rejecting the inventor path
jokes.punchline: Lists 'science-fiction cartoon writer' as useful career while being in a science-fiction cartoon
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta joke that breaks fourth wall while maintaining character voice

jokes.text: In your life, your only half-decent invention was me. And I didn't turn out like you wanted either.
jokes.setup: Cubert dismissing the Professor's work
jokes.punchline: Claims to be his best invention while simultaneously being a failure
jokes.explanation: Dark but clever self-awareness joke that cuts deep while being funny

jokes.text: Turns out I forgot to cook that chicken.
jokes.setup: Celebrity chef checking on diners
jokes.punchline: Casually admits to potentially dangerous cooking mistake
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of serious food safety violation as minor oversight

jokes.text: You see, I've been lying about my age. I'm not actually 150. I'm 160.
jokes.setup: Confession about lying about age
jokes.punchline: Reveals he lied to seem younger, not older
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that subverts expectations about age deception

jokes.text: Oh, vanity, thy name is Professor Farnsworth.
jokes.setup: His admission of vanity about age
jokes.punchline: Misquotes Shakespeare while being vain about his own vanity
jokes.explanation: Literary reference joke that shows character's pretentiousness

jokes.text: Goodbye, cruel world! Goodbye, cruel lamp. Goodbye, cruel velvet drapes... lined with what appears to be some sort of cruel muslin... and the cute little pompon curtain pull cords... cruel though they may be...
jokes.setup: Dramatic farewell to cruel world
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted by interior decorating details while maintaining dramatic tone
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that shows Farnsworth's tendency to get distracted even during dramatic moments

jokes.text: I know you're all upset, especially Bender. Well, life goes on. Except for you!
jokes.setup: Acknowledging everyone's sadness about his departure
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts sympathy with dark reminder about death
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor that shows Farnsworth's inability to comfort even when trying

jokes.text: I'm sure he's just made a cutting remark... but he doesn't know I taped over his soap operas to record this. You bastard!
jokes.setup: Professor assumes Bender made cutting remark
jokes.punchline: Reveals he ruined Bender's shows, causing the actual angry reaction
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup and payoff that creates the reaction it predicts

jokes.text: You're his uncle, dummy. He takes after you! Yeah, well, what?
jokes.setup: Leela corrects Fry's logic about inheritance
jokes.punchline: Fry's confused non-response shows he doesn't understand
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's confusion and weak comebacks

jokes.text: As you probably don't know... odors are made up of particles that can't travel through space.
jokes.setup: Cubert being condescending about science
jokes.punchline: Delivers actual scientific fact in arrogant way
jokes.explanation: Shows Cubert's know-it-all personality while setting up science to be ignored

jokes.text: I'm zeroing in on him. BENGAY. Mothballs! Letters to the editor! It's the professor!
jokes.setup: Using smell-scope to track Professor
jokes.punchline: Identifies him through stereotypical old person smells
jokes.explanation: Good stereotypical old person joke with specific details

jokes.text: No, we're not, you little bed wetter. Sorry.
jokes.setup: Leela says robots are good at keeping secrets
jokes.punchline: Robot immediately contradicts this by insulting and apologizing
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment where robot proves the opposite of claimed ability

jokes.text: My God... the illusion is so perfect I almost forgot I was looking at an idiot!
jokes.setup: Praising Fry's old man disguise
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment that insults while praising
jokes.explanation: Good backhanded compliment structure with Leela's typical Fry-bashing

jokes.text: I'd like to see them find it.
jokes.setup: Discussion of DNA sample needed
jokes.punchline: Fry's confidence suggests his DNA is well-hidden (implying small anatomy)
jokes.explanation: Basic innuendo joke that relies on crude implication

jokes.text: Why do I have to be the hump? Because you're too ugly to be a wart.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg complaining about disguise role
jokes.punchline: Leela's harsh but logical explanation based on appearance
jokes.explanation: Good insult with internal logic - mean but funny character interaction

jokes.text: Hey, you kids! Get off the lawn! Old people are often concerned that there are children on their lawns.
jokes.setup: Fry doing stereotypical old person voice
jokes.punchline: Robot's matter-of-fact confirmation of the stereotype
jokes.explanation: Standard old person stereotype with robotic literal confirmation

jokes.text: Got a hot, steaming batch right here. We only needed one cell. Keep the change, buddy!
jokes.setup: Robot asks for DNA sample
jokes.punchline: Leela provides excessive sample with overly casual delivery
jokes.explanation: Crude bodily function humor with awkward over-delivery

jokes.text: I've got 'talking hump syndrome.' Ah, T.H.S.
jokes.setup: Robot questions the hump talking
jokes.punchline: Made-up medical condition with acronym treatment
jokes.explanation: Good absurd medical excuse that the robot immediately accepts with acronym

jokes.text: So this is where they stick old people! It's horrific! It does keep them from driving.
jokes.setup: Horror at elderly treatment facility
jokes.punchline: Amy finds practical benefit in the horrible situation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about elderly stereotypes with Amy's characteristic shallow perspective

jokes.text: Seize them! Get them! I mean, seize them!
jokes.setup: Robot trying to give dramatic arrest command
jokes.punchline: Self-corrects to use proper vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Decent robot humor about programming and proper vocabulary usage.

jokes.text: Your social security check is late! Stuff costs more than it used to! Young people use curse words!
jokes.setup: Trying to shock the professor awake
jokes.punchline: List of stereotypical old person concerns
jokes.explanation: Good escalating list of old person complaints with realistic stereotypes.

jokes.text: The engines don't move the ship at all! The ship stays where it is, and the engines move the universe around it!
jokes.setup: Explaining how the impossible engines work
jokes.punchline: Completely backwards logic that somehow makes sense in context
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist science fiction concept that perfectly captures show's approach to impossible technology

jokes.text: That's a complete load. Nothing's a complete load. Not if you can imagine it.
jokes.setup: Cubert's scientific skepticism
jokes.punchline: Fry's optimistic philosophy that contradicts reason
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier theme while showing character growth in Fry

jokes.text: I was at a facility in Florida... with hundreds of other old people. All day we'd play bingo, eat oatmeal and wait for our children to call. It's more horrible than anything I could imagine.
jokes.setup: Professor describes his virtual reality experience
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was essentially a modern nursing home - the most horrific fate
jokes.explanation: Dark social commentary disguised as sci-fi horror - the real nightmare is mundane elderly care

jokes.text: Oh, don't worry, son. You will. Incidentally, read up on the condition known as wandering bladder. Why? No reason. No reason at all.
jokes.setup: Cubert wants to be like his father
jokes.punchline: Professor's ominous hint about future medical problems
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor ending that suggests Cubert will inherit more than just personality traits

tmdb_overview: Professor Farnsworth unveils his clone, Cubert Farnsworth, whom he plans to leave everything to upon his retirement. But Cubert wants no part of Farnsworth's lifestyle, prompting the professor into a premature retirement on the Near-Death Star.
